source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/SpatialExtremes/SpatialGEVAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- X12 structural oracle. SpatialGEVAnalysis drives a seeded DEMCzs MCMC over the hierarchical 3-site x 15-obs SpatialGEV model then turns the posterior into per-site GEV/quantile bands and a regional site-averaged frequency curve on the 25 default exceedance ordinates. site_count == 3 and curve_length == 25. The site bands + regional curve are posterior means/quantiles, so they inherit the chaotic sensitivity. The seeded DEMCzs posterior is CHAOTICALLY sensitive across the C# and C++ MersenneTwister streams: the deterministic model log-likelihood + MLE match C#/C++ to rel 1e-8 (proven by the model_estimation fixtures), but the 300-iteration seeded chain MAP diverges at ~1e-6 (model-density ULP differences amplified over the chain -- inherent chaotic sensitivity, NOT a port bug; see docs/upstream-csharp-issues.md). Per the chaotic-sensitivity rule the posterior-dependent curve is NOT pinned (never oracle_skip-masked, never a loosened tolerance); the fixture asserts the deterministic structural invariants that reproduce bit-identically across C#/C++/R/Python. Emitter-verified against the real RMC.BestFit class via tools/oracle_emitter.
